themes.topic: AEHR's Strong Earnings and AI/Optical/Memory Testing Outlook
themes.summary: Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) reported strong Q4 earnings and an impressive FY2027 revenue guidance of $130-$150M, driven by accelerating demand for wafer-level burn-in from AI processors, silicon photonics, and new opportunities in HBM/NAND memory testing.

mentions.text: I’m gradually adding to my positions because I believe the three signals from $AEHR ’s latest earnings report will soon put many of the current concerns surrounding the optical market to rest. 🥹

50%?! $AEHR 

Insane. Some test names moving with it, mostly $TRT 

Hopefully this is the setup that we will get with many of the optics companies.

I posted a full breakdown of all the things that stood out to me on the call if you'd like to read it here:

https://t.co/4Rn4ZkCX7

$AEHR back up +36.4% today off earnings!

2027 guide: $130-$150m (160-200% growth) from 2026 revenue.  Sees opportunity to guide higher (assumes no memory revenue or little to none from newly benchmarked AI customer).

Q4 bookings: $60.7M, effective backlog is $100.6M.

- Lead AI

$AEHR 🤯
"That is, with strong momentum and a record backlog heading into fiscal 27. We're expecting revenue of between $130 million and $150 million, representing 2.6 times to three times the just completed fiscal 26 revenue non-GAAP pre-tax pre-tax profitability at these levels

Wow, unreal $AEHR Q4 earnings and FY2027 outlook, +26% after-hours.

I have high conviction that Aehr is well positioned for many years of very strong growth:

- Multiple customers entering/expanding production
- Huge backlog
- Opportunities in new markets e.g. NAND and HBM appli

themes.topic: ASML Strong Earnings, Raised Guidance, and Capacity Expansion
themes.summary: ASML reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and significantly boosted its full-year net sales guidance to €43-45 billion, driven by strong demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and plans to increase both low NA EUV and DUV immersion capacity by 30% for 2027 and potentially 2028.

mentions.text: ASML reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results, driven by continued demand for semiconductor manufacturing equipment. The June wholesale inflation report came in below expectations, potentially easing concerns about inflationary pressures in the broader economy. These dev

$ASML Installed Base services revenue is growing quickly and is becoming a larger part of the overall business. Services revenue will only keep growing as the installed base increases. I don’t feel like analysts or the market are giving the company enough credit for this. https:/

themes.topic: TSMC Q2 Earnings Expectations and AI Outlook
themes.summary: TSMC's Q2 earnings are expected to show a 59% net profit increase and record gross/operating margins, with investors keenly awaiting updates on AI demand health, CoWoS capacity, N2/N3 ramp, and potential price increases, alongside a raised 2024-2029 AI semiconductor revenue CAGR forecast of 70-80%.

mentions.text:
TSMC 2nd quarter (Q2) conference on Thursday (7/16), media report expectations:
Q2 revenue NT$1.27 trillion (already known, US$ not known)
-Net profit seen up 59% to NT$632.6 billion (US$19.6B), 10th straight quarter of growth
-Gross margin could exceed 68%
-Operating margin seen

themes.topic: Memory Market Dynamics: DRAM Price Pushback & Strong NAND Demand
themes.summary: Customers are pushing back against 30% DRAM price increases, leading to a lowered Q3 DRAM price growth forecast, while NAND demand is strong due to KV cache offloading and substitution for expensive DRAM.

mentions.text:
Excerpt from GFHK’s monthly call: Memory sector commentary

Customers are pushing back strongly against price increases approaching 30%, leading us to slightly lower our forecast for DRAM price growth in the third quarter.

By contrast, we are becoming more positive on NAND. Dema

themes.topic: Samsung Considers Outsourcing Google 2nm TPU I/O Die Design
themes.summary: Samsung Electronics is considering outsourcing the back-end design for Google's 2nm TPU I/O die due to a staff shortage from surging 2nm foundry demand, with Google's compute processor to be made by TSMC at 1.4nm and the I/O die by Samsung at 2nm.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: SEMI Raises Global Semiconductor Equipment Sales Forecasts
themes.summary: SEMI has raised its global semiconductor equipment sales forecasts for the next three years, projecting $165.9 billion in 2026, driven by strong AI-related demand for leading-edge logic, memory chips, and advanced packaging.

themes.topic: PJM Capacity Auction Reveals Structural Power Shortfall
themes.summary: The 2028/2029 PJM capacity auction confirmed a persistent structural shortfall in dispatchable and reliably accredited capacity, with the clearing price hitting an administratively imposed ceiling of $325/MW-day despite an estimated unconstrained price of $554.72/MW-day, highlighting long-term scarcity.

themes.summary: DeepSeek is raising $7.4 billion at a $71 billion pre-money valuation with nearly $500 million ARR, and is preparing for an IPO on Shanghai’s STAR Market as early as next year, while also seeking US dollar-denominated capital from overseas investors.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: Apple's Potential AI Chip Acquisitions and Capex Increase
themes.summary: Apple is reportedly looking to acquire AI chip companies to bolster its own AI infrastructure, potentially leading to a significant increase in its AI capex spend, which could be bullish for related sectors like optical networking and foundries.

A possible scenario is that they revise capex largely upward for their own AI

themes.topic: TSMC Pushing Back on ASML Equipment Pricing
themes.summary: TSMC is reportedly pushing back against ASML's plans to raise equipment prices, although ASML intends to proceed with the price increases.
